punt_reason: Section block is 2186 words against target_max 700 (3.12× over). Subtraction profile: sentence_complexity -5 (22 sentences >35 words). A Category A sentence-split pass could bring understandability from 5 to ~9 (sentence_complexity -5 → -1), giving composite 0.4·0.6 + 0.6·9 = 5.6 (delta +2.4 clears the 1.5 gate). Mechanically rewrite-eligible. Punting from THIS batch for capacity reasons: this is the third 2000+ word section block in the top 5 by composite, and the conservative-cap-5 batch has already absorbed two such surfaces as PUNTs plus one detail_verdict_text rewrite (WIN-010). Pushing a same-shape PUNT-cluster to operator without at least one section-block rewrite attempted is the right first-run posture — let operator see the pattern (top batch dominated by oversized section blocks where length is the dominant uncorrectable defect) before any individual section-block RW lands in curmudgeon. Next Phase 2 run (2026-05-18) will attempt this surface as an RW unless operator has signaled an editorial split.
category_gap_signal: Same pattern as PUNT-001 and PUNT-002 — Category A can lift understandability but cannot fix length=0.6 in a 3× over-length section_details_block. Cluster of three top-5 surfaces all section_details_blocks at 2.2-6.0× target_max is a calibration signal: either the target_max=700 is too tight for content-dense parts (which should then be on content-dense-surfaces.json with the 2.0× multiplier), or the editorial expectation is that these sections should be split. Operator review of which.
recommendation_to_operator: Either (a) add part4.html#p4-427-station-coverage to content-dense-surfaces.json (4+ station-by-station sub-claims, 2+ citation clusters per paragraph — content-dense pattern fits) to elevate target_max to 1400 (still 1.56× over, but length score would lift to ~3.8), or (b) editorial split into per-station-cluster sub-blocks. Option (a) is the minimal-touch fix that doesn't require structural change; option (b) is the more durable answer if the section's argument naturally chunks. Either reduces this surface's eligibility for Phase 2 rewrite to a tractable Category A pass on a single bounded sub-block.
